summary: Tiny player interface type-correctness fix: Player_GetControllerIndex was changed from returning s8 to returning u8 in both src/melee/pl/player.c and src/melee/pl/player.h. The PR had no review comments and only changed the function signature, keeping implementation body otherwise unchanged.
what_changed: Changed Player_GetControllerIndex return type in src/melee/pl/player.c from s8 to u8.
Changed the matching declaration in src/melee/pl/player.h from s8 Player_GetControllerIndex(s32 slot) to u8 Player_GetControllerIndex(s32 slot).
No behavioral logic changes are visible in the diff excerpt; the function still declares a local s8 controller_index, obtains a StaticPlayer pointer, and calls Player_CheckSlot(slot).
smart_moves: Kept the implementation signature and public header declaration synchronized, avoiding ABI/API mismatch between player.c and player.h.
Limited the change to the narrow type issue instead of touching surrounding player interface functions, reducing matching and regression risk.
Used an unsigned return type for controller index, which is consistent with controller/player slot index values being non-negative interface data.
decomp_lessons: Small signedness corrections in public headers matter because many decompiled callsites inherit types from declarations; fixing the interface can prevent propagation of incorrect signed comparisons or casts.
When correcting a function type, update both the C definition and header prototype in the same PR.
Even if an internal local remains signed in the current decompiled body, the exported return type may still need to reflect the real API or observed usage.
naming_conventions: Player interface functions use PascalCase with a Player_ prefix, e.g. Player_GetControllerIndex, Player_SetControllerIndex, Player_GetCostumeId, Player_SetCostumeId.
Slot parameters are named slot and typed s32.
Controller index parameters in nearby setter code are named controller_index.
Player module files live under src/melee/pl/ with player.c and player.h providing implementation and interface.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly diff or matching explanation was included in the PR context.
The visible tactic was a minimal signature signedness correction, likely intended to preserve existing matching while correcting interface typing.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find other player interface functions where return signedness differs between implementation, header, and actual value domain.
Search for callsites of Player_GetControllerIndex that may rely on signed s8 behavior or should be updated to u8-compatible comparisons.
Check whether the local controller_index variable inside Player_GetControllerIndex should also become u8 in a future matching-safe cleanup.
